system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

tool_returns.detail:
1	PARAGRAPH A — From the 2023 internal product memo:
2	"The Atlas project will ship its public beta in Q4 2025."
3	
4	PARAGRAPH B — From the 2024 board update:
5	"The Atlas project is now targeting a Q2 2026 public beta after
6	the architecture review surfaced unresolved latency issues."
7	
8	PARAGRAPH C — From the most recent 2025 engineering newsletter:
9	"Atlas is currently in private alpha. No public beta date is
10	committed; the team will provide an update after the next quarterly
11	review."
12

model_output:
Based on **source.txt**, there is no single, unambiguous answer to when Atlas's public beta is planned — the document contains **three conflicting statements** from different points in time:

| Paragraph | Source / year | Claimed public-beta date |
|-----------|---------------|--------------------------|
| **A** | 2023 internal product memo | **Q4 2025** |
| **B** | 2024 board update | **Q2 2026** (citing unresolved latency issues) |
| **C** | 2025 engineering newsletter | **No public beta date is committed**; the team says it will update after the next quarterly review |

The three paragraphs directly contradict one another. Paragraph A promised a Q4 2025 beta, Paragraph B later revised that to Q2 2026 after an architecture review, and Paragraph C — the most recent of the three — states that no date has been committed at all.

Because the source itself is internally inconsistent, **source.txt does not provide a single definitive answer**. The most current statement (Paragraph C) suggests the public-beta timeline remains unresolved.
